title: Partner-run legibility validation study (consented, aggregate, de-identified)
context: Success metric O8 in PLAN.md calls for documented, honest legibility validation — including null/mixed results. This project defaults to relying on published/partner research to avoid human-subjects and PII burden, but if a partner offers to run a consented, aggregate, de-identified study on the shipped accessibility family, this task coordinates and documents the outcome. PII constraints are strict: no disability status or health data may be tied to an identifiable person. Not started without a confirmed partner offering to run the study.
objective: Coordinate and document a partner-run legibility validation study of the shipped accessibility-first Latin family (and/or non-Latin accessibility face if available), covering consented, aggregate, de-identified reader feedback; report results honestly including null or mixed findings.
acceptanceCriteria: A named partner offers to conduct the study; verifiedNeed remains false until this offer is confirmed.
Study is designed and run by the partner, not by this project; this project coordinates logistics and documents the outcome.
Study protocol includes informed consent from participants; no disability status, health data, or personally identifiable information is tied to individual results.
Results are reported aggregate and de-identified; no identifiable person's disability or reading difference status is published.
Findings are published honestly: null or mixed results are reported as such per the O8 honesty clause; no suppression of unfavorable data.
If results are null/mixed, the family is reframed as 'high-legibility, openly licensed' rather than presenting the findings as positive.
Document is CC-BY-4.0; study methodology and limitations documented.
resources: PLAN.md (section 4: success metrics — O8 honesty clause)
PLAN.md (section 7: privacy/PII — disability status as sensitive PII)
TASKS.md (backlog — fonts-eval-310: only if a partner offers)
output: A published partner-run legibility study report (CC-BY-4.0) documenting aggregate, de-identified results from a consented reader study, including honest reporting of null or mixed findings, merged into the project repository.
